q: Statements: All pens are books. All books are papers. Conclusions: I. All pens are papers. II. Some papers are pens.
DESCRIBE: Pens ⊆ Books ⊆ Papers. Therefore all pens are papers and some papers are pens.

q: Statements: All cats are animals. Some animals are dogs. Conclusions: I. Some dogs are cats. II. No dog is a cat.
DESCRIBE: The relationship between cats and dogs is unknown. Either overlap exists or not. Hence either I or II follows.

q: Statements: Some flowers are roses. All roses are red. Conclusions: I. Some flowers are red. II. All flowers are red.
DESCRIBE: Some flowers are roses and all roses are red, so some flowers are red. All flowers being red is not definite.

q: Statements: No chair is a table. All tables are furniture. Conclusions: I. No chair is furniture. II. Some furniture are tables.
DESCRIBE: No relation is given between chairs and furniture. Tables are furniture, so some furniture are tables.

q: Statements: All mangoes are fruits. Some fruits are sweet. Conclusions: I. Some mangoes are sweet. II. All sweet things are fruits.
DESCRIBE: No direct relation between mangoes and sweet fruits. Also all sweet things being fruits is not given.

q: Statements: Some A are B. Some B are C. Conclusions: I. Some A are C. II. Some C are B.
DESCRIBE: Some B are C implies some C are B. A and C connection is not definite.

q: Statements: All engineers are graduates. All graduates are employed. Conclusions: I. All engineers are employed. II. Some employed are engineers.
DESCRIBE: Engineers ⊆ Graduates ⊆ Employed. Both conclusions follow.

q: Statements: No bird is a mammal. Some mammals are pets. Conclusions: I. No pet is a bird. II. Some pets are mammals.
DESCRIBE: Some mammals are pets means some pets are mammals. No pet is bird cannot be concluded.

q: Statements: All apples are fruits. No fruit is a vegetable. Conclusions: I. No apple is a vegetable. II. Some fruits are apples.
DESCRIBE: Apples are fruits and fruits are not vegetables. Also apples exist, so some fruits are apples.

q: Statements: Some students are athletes. All athletes are healthy. Conclusions: I. Some students are healthy. II. All students are healthy.
DESCRIBE: Students who are athletes are healthy. All students healthy is not definite.

q: Statements: All trucks are vehicles. Some vehicles are buses. Conclusions: I. Some buses are trucks. II. All trucks are vehicles.
DESCRIBE: Only the direct conclusion 'All trucks are vehicles' follows.

q: Statements: Some books are novels. All novels are interesting. Conclusions: I. Some books are interesting. II. All books are interesting.
DESCRIBE: Books that are novels are interesting. Not all books are necessarily interesting.

q: Statements: No pen is a pencil. Some pencils are erasers. Conclusions: I. Some erasers are pencils. II. No eraser is a pen.
DESCRIBE: Some pencils are erasers implies some erasers are pencils. No relation between erasers and pens.

q: Statements: All teachers are educated. Some educated are writers. Conclusions: I. Some writers are teachers. II. Some educated are teachers.
DESCRIBE: Teachers are educated, so some educated are teachers. Writers and teachers relation is uncertain.

q: Statements: All cars are machines. No machine is living. Conclusions: I. No car is living. II. Some machines are cars.
DESCRIBE: Cars ⊆ Machines and Machines ∩ Living = Ø. Both conclusions follow.

q: Statements: Some doctors are surgeons. All surgeons are specialists. Conclusions: I. Some doctors are specialists. II. All doctors are specialists.
DESCRIBE: Doctors who are surgeons are specialists. All doctors being specialists is not given.

q: Statements: All laptops are computers. All computers are electronic devices. Conclusions: I. All laptops are electronic devices. II. Some electronic devices are laptops.
DESCRIBE: Laptops ⊆ Computers ⊆ Electronic Devices. Both conclusions follow.

q: Statements: Some fruits are oranges. Some oranges are sweet. Conclusions: I. Some fruits are sweet. II. Some sweet things are oranges.
DESCRIBE: Some oranges are sweet means some sweet things are oranges. Fruits and sweet relation is not definite.

q: Statements: No A is B. Some B are C. Conclusions: I. Some C are B. II. No C is A.
DESCRIBE: Some B are C implies some C are B. No conclusion about C and A.

q: Statements: All actors are artists. Some artists are singers. Conclusions: I. Some singers are artists. II. Some singers are actors.
DESCRIBE: Some artists are singers implies some singers are artists. Actors and singers relation is uncertain.
